title: Sara, Eva & The Great Backyard Burger Heist!
logline: Papa fires up the grill on a sunny Saturday in his 'WORLD'S OKAYEST DAD' apron. Sara helps with buns, Eva carries ketchup-packets like a tiny waitress. The instant Papa steps inside to answer the doorbell, Joe (the Pomeranian) pulls off the slowest, most magnificent slow-motion food heist in dog history — and Papa returns convinced he must have DAYDREAMED the whole cookout. The girls (and the kid watching) know exactly where the burgers went. Cliffhanger: Joe locks eyes with the camera while Papa fires up grill round 3. What's tomorrow's meal? PANCAKES?!
formulaReference: Bluey-style 'family chaos comedy + dog-thief gag' — confident-Papa setup → fourth-wall conspiracy with viewer (kids see what Papa doesn't) → slow-mo musical heist centerpiece → gaslit-Papa confusion → girls-as-witnesses reveal → physical-comedy outburst → reconciliation → cliffhanger that sets up ep12 (pancakes). Sequel-tier callback to ep04 'Joe's Secret Stash' (channel #1 hero @ 16.8k views, 2× next).
expectedCreditsBreakdown: 14 normal clips × 90 cr = 1260 + 2 parent-activity 15s clips × 135 cr = 270 + 2 render specs (A,B) × 90 cr = 180 → 1710 cr base; +25% buffer ≈ 2138 cr realistic ceiling. Comfortably under 2200 abort threshold.

newBoundElements.purpose: Backyard staged for cookout — black gas grill + red-and-white checkered picnic table + condiments + bushes Joe hides in. Used in clips 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 13, 14, 16.

Charcoal-grey 'WORLD'S OKAYEST DAD' apron with cartoon dad-mustache chef icon. Worn by Papa in clips 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 9, 13, 14, 15. Text-prop — apply English-text-anchor in every prompt; if rendered text drifts, post-process clip 1 close-up via ffmpeg + Pillow text overlay (memory rule #17/18).
Round ceramic platter with 3 hot dogs + 4 burger patties (cooked, grill-marked). Used in clips 6 (full, on table), 13 (graveyard around Joe — empty wrappers and crumbs implied).

reusedBoundElements: Sara, Eva, Mama, Papa, Joe — character avatars
backyard — clips 10 (girls in grass laughing), 11 (Joe in bush corner), 15 (parent-activity tackle-hug in grass)

outro.note: 0_song.mp4 sorts last via parseInt-Infinity hack — keep (memory rule #23)

music.policy: ALL music is Suno — Kling renders dialogue + ambient ONLY. No music phrases inside any clip prompt (memory rule #24).

musicVideoBlocks.placement: between clip 6 (Joe creeps out of bush, eyes locked on grill) and clip 7 (Papa returns confused) — Joe's slow-motion heist set to dramatic surf-rock heist music. Trimmed to 20s post-ep11 review (was 60s, user said too long).
musicVideoBlocks.mood: Comically epic slow-motion crime — bass-heavy surf-rock with jazz-flute flourishes, the canine equivalent of an Ocean's Eleven montage

musicVideoBlocks.placement: between clip 15 (parent-activity tackle-hug in grass) and clip 16 (cliffhanger) — Papa-Sara-Eva sock-hop celebration around the grill, apron flapping
musicVideoBlocks.mood: Goofy dad-rock celebration — upbeat 50s rock-and-roll, hand-claps, cheerful brass, kids cheering 'GO PAPA GO!'

renders.A: content/episodes/ep11/A.json — Joe solo slow-mo heist, designed-grill backdrop, 60s loop. Joe is the SOLO STAR of this music video.
renders.B: content/episodes/ep11/B.json — Sara+Eva+Papa trio dance, designed-cookout backdrop with apron + grill smoke, 30s loop

arc.name: HOOK — Apron close-up + grill ignition
arc.purpose: Establishes Saturday-cookout premise + 'WORLD'S OKAYEST DAD' joke. Papa's confidence sets up his fall.

arc.name: Sara helps with buns
arc.purpose: Sara lining up burger buns on the picnic table — domestic helper beat.

arc.name: Eva — tiny waitress with ketchup packets
arc.purpose: Eva carries a bowl of ketchup-packets out, total tiny-waitress confidence. Comic gold.

arc.name: PARENT-ACTIVITY 1 (15s) — Patty-flip lesson
arc.purpose: Papa shows Sara, then Eva, how to flip ONE patty each on the grill. 3-beat structure. (memory: lesson_papa_play_scene_per_episode.md — Papa-active-cooking-with-girls hybrid)

arc.name: Mama (offscreen): doorbell — Papa exits
arc.purpose: Mama's voice from inside calls Papa to the door. He sets down spatula and jogs in.

arc.name: AUDIENCE-ASK + Joe creeps out
arc.purpose: Empty grill-area beat. JOE the Pomeranian creeps out from behind the bushes, eyes locked on the burger-platter. He gives the camera a tiny conspiratorial look (silent fourth-wall acknowledgment).

arc.name: 🎵 MUSIC VIDEO A — Slow-Mo Burger Heist (60s)
arc.purpose: Joe's full slow-motion heist set to surf-rock. The comedic centerpiece. Render A under 'Slow-Mo Burger Heist' — Joe solo.

arc.name: AUDIENCE-ASK — Papa returns confused
arc.purpose: Papa returns to empty grill + empty plates. Stares confused. Looks DIRECTLY at camera, baffled: 'Did I cook anything today? Or did I just DREAM the whole thing?' Top-tier engagement beat.

arc.name: Papa starts new round of patties
arc.purpose: Papa scratches head, mutters about losing his mind, slaps a fresh round of patties on the grill.

arc.name: Mama returns — confusion deepens
arc.purpose: Mama walks back out, sees Papa cooking AGAIN, confused: '...weren't you just here?' Papa shrugs helplessly.

arc.name: AUDIENCE-ASK — Cut to girls collapsed laughing
arc.purpose: Sara + Eva collapsed in grass dying laughing, hands on tummies. Sara turns to camera: 'Kids — did YOU see where the FIRST burgers went?!' Massive curiosity-gap payoff (audience knows, Papa doesn't).

arc.name: Joe in the bush corner — evidence!
arc.purpose: Joe in the corner of the yard, hot dog hanging from his snout, ketchup smeared all over his fluffy face. Reveal of where the burgers went.

arc.name: AUDIENCE-ASK — Eva: should we tell Papa?
arc.purpose: Eva pops up in grass, finger to her lips, looking at camera with naughty grin: 'Should we tell Papa?' (beat — Sara nods solemnly) Eva (yells): 'PAPA — LOOK!'

arc.name: Papa sees the burger graveyard
arc.purpose: Papa turns at Eva's yell, follows her pointing finger, JAW DROPS as he spots Joe and the empty platter / empty wrappers. Apoplectic recognition.

arc.name: JOOOOE! — mmf?
arc.purpose: Papa: 'JOOOOE!' Joe (mouth full of hot dog, innocent eyes): 'mmf?'

arc.name: PARENT-ACTIVITY 2 (15s) — Lunge, slip, tackle-hug
arc.purpose: Papa lunges (off-frame at Joe), slips on a ketchup-packet, face-plants in the grass with theatrical 'ARGH!', Sara + Eva pile-tackle-hug Papa giggling. 3-beat structure. (Joe NOT bound here — clip is 3-foreground-char to avoid ghost trap.)

arc.name: 🎵 MUSIC VIDEO B — World's Okayest Dad (30s)
arc.purpose: Render B under 'World's Okayest Dad' — Sara+Eva+Papa sock-hop trio dance celebration around the grill.

arc.name: CLIFFHANGER + AUDIENCE-ASK — Pancakes?!
arc.purpose: Papa fires up grill round 3, ONE eye locked on Joe behind the bush. Joe calmly licks his lips, makes EYE CONTACT with camera. Eva turns to camera: 'What do YOU think Papa should make tomorrow?' Sara whispers: '...PANCAKES?!' Direct setup for ep12 (Mama's pancake morning — already in backlog). Cut to black.

audienceEngagement.rule: memory: lesson_fourth_wall_audience_engagement.md — 2-4 fourth-wall direct-to-camera 'ask the kid' beats per episode; final cliffhanger MUST be a camera-ask

beats.framing: Joe glances at camera with conspiratorial sparkle in eye before stealing food — non-verbal fourth-wall break

beats.line: Did I cook anything today? Or did I just DREAM the whole thing?
Kids — did YOU see where the FIRST burgers went?!
What do YOU think Papa should make tomorrow?

rules.klingPromptMustNotContainReason: All music is Suno-mixed in assemble (memory rule #24).
rules.motionTowardForbidden: No 'walks in/toward', 'approaches', 'moves to(ward)', 'heads to/in', 'walks up' — these spawn duplicates when another character is anchored. Use static placement only. (memory: lesson_kling_motion_verbs_duplicate.md)
rules.max3ForegroundChars: Hard cap 3 foreground-anchored characters per clip. ALL ep11 clips comply — clip 4 (Papa+Sara+Eva = 3), clip 15 (Papa+Sara+Eva = 3, Joe NOT bound here), clip 13/14 (Papa+Joe = 2, even with platter visible).
rules.negativePromptRequired: duplicate character, twin, clone, two of the same, mirrored figure, second father, second mother, two Papa, two Mama, identical adults, extra people, third child, second sister, second sara, second eva, two eva, duplicate eva, duplicate dog, second dog, two pomeranians, mirror reflection, family members in the background, additional children, extra child in background, second blonde child, ghost figure, shadow of person, three arms, extra hand, anatomy error
rules.characterNamesInDialogue: Drop the bound character's name from other characters' dialogue (memory: rule from ep01). Note: 'PAPA — LOOK!' (Eva yelling) and 'JOOOOE!' (Papa yelling) are exceptions — both names refer to the FOREGROUND-anchored characters in those clips, not absent ones, so the duplicate-char trigger doesn't apply.
rules.audienceEngagementMin: ≥2 fourth-wall direct-to-camera 'ask the kid' beats per episode (final cliffhanger is one). ep11 ships 4 question-beats + 1 silent Joe-wink. memory: lesson_fourth_wall_audience_engagement.md
rules.textPropPostProcess: Apron text 'WORLD'S OKAYEST DAD' relies on Kling's best-effort with English-text-anchor language. If clip 1 (apron close-up) renders garbled text, ffmpeg+Pillow overlay using the canonical papas_apron.png frame (memory rule #17/18, lesson_episode_thumbnail_recipe.md technique).
rules.joeIsCanonicalChar: Joe = the family Pomeranian. Bound character in Kling library (joe_front.png/joe_3q.png/joe_profile.png/joe_sheet.png). Continuity callback to ep04 'Joe's Secret Stash' (channel #1 hero, 16.8k views).

clips.title: HOOK — Apron close-up + grill ignition
clips.prompt: Sunny Saturday afternoon medium-close shot in @backyard-grill-area, golden-hour light, faint heat-shimmer rising from the grill. @Papa stands CENTER-FRAME at the grill, just finishing tying the waist strings of @papas-apron behind his back, then proudly smooths the front of the apron flat across his chest so the printed text reads loud and clear toward the camera: WORLD'S OKAYEST DAD. The apron's cartoon dad-mustache chef icon and the printed letters are all clearly readable, English text only, large clear printed Roman alphabet, NO foreign characters. Papa grins huge, picks up a metal spatula from the grill's side hook, and waggles it cheerfully. He flips a perfectly-formed hamburger patty on the hot grates with a satisfying *sizzle* and a small puff of cheerful steam. Papa (booming, proud chef energy): "Saturday burgers — DAD MODE: ENGAGED!" He hums tunelessly, deeply satisfied with himself.

clips.title: Sara helps line up the burger buns on the picnic table
clips.prompt: Wide cheerful shot of @backyard-grill-area in golden-hour light. @Papa already on the LEFT side at the hot grill wearing @papas-apron, flipping a hamburger patty with the spatula, faint smoke wisps rising. @Sara already standing on the RIGHT side at the red-and-white checkered picnic table, hair in cheerful pigtails, carefully lining up fluffy round burger buns one-by-one in a neat row with intense five-year-old concentration, tongue poking out the corner of her mouth. Sara (proudly to herself): "Bun. Bun. Bun. Bun. PERFECT row of buns." Papa (calling over from the grill, grinning): "My sous-chef! Captain of the Bun Squad!" Sara (puffing up her chest, hands on hips for two seconds): "Captain of the BUN SQUAD, reporting for duty!" She giggles and goes right back to bun-lining.

clips.title: Eva — tiny waitress with a bowl of ketchup packets
clips.prompt: Cheerful low-angle medium shot in @backyard-grill-area, sunny afternoon. @Eva already standing CENTER-FRAME on the grass next to the picnic table, curly blonde hair with rainbow bow, wearing a small kid's polka-dot sundress, holding a bright white plastic BOWL with both hands carefully balanced in front of her, the bowl piled high with little red and white ketchup packets. Eva walks with intense five-year-old waitress importance — taking tiny careful steps in place to keep the bowl level — chin up, shoulders back, eyes super-serious. Eva (in her best fancy-restaurant voice): "One bowl of KEH-CHOOP for the BUR-GER guests, please!" She bows her head once formally, lifts the bowl with a tiny dramatic flourish, then cracks up giggling at her own joke and bobs her shoulders. Eva (grinning, sing-song): "I'm the FANCIEST waitress in the WHOLE backyard!"

clips.title: PARENT-ACTIVITY (15s) — Papa teaches Sara and Eva to flip ONE patty each
clips.prompt: Wide cinematic medium shot of @backyard-grill-area, sunny golden-hour light, faint heat-shimmer above the grates. @Papa already in the CENTER at the grill wearing @papas-apron, holding a long metal spatula in one hand, big proud teaching grin. @Sara already standing on the LEFT next to Papa, on a small upturned wooden block to reach grill height, both hands gripping a kid-sized spatula. @Eva already standing on the RIGHT at the other side of Papa on her own block, holding her own kid-sized spatula. (BEAT 1 — 0-5s) Papa (cheerful coach voice): "Okay champs — gentle slide UNDER, then a confident FLIP — like THIS!" He demonstrates with his spatula, flipping a juicy patty cleanly with a satisfying *sizzle*. Sara (eyes saucers): "OOH!" Eva (gasping, hand to mouth): "Pro chef Papa!" (BEAT 2 — 5-10s) Sara goes first — slowly slides her spatula under a patty, scrunches her face in concentration, FLIPS — the patty lands cleanly with a sizzle. Sara (fists pumping in the air for one second): "YESSS! Bun Squad CAN COOK!" Papa beams down at her, totally proud, gives her a giant thumbs-up. (BEAT 3 — 10-15s) Eva tries next — slides her spatula under, flips with WAY too much enthusiasm, the patty rotates almost end-over-end and lands askew with a loud *SPLAT* of grease. Eva freezes one second wide-eyed, then breaks into a triumphant grin. Eva (arms up, triumphant): "BURGER GYMNASTICS! TEN POINTS!" Papa laughs and ruffles her hair. Papa (grinning): "My grill team, ladies and gentlemen!"

clips.title: Mama (offscreen) — 'doorbell!' — Papa exits
clips.prompt: Medium shot of @backyard-grill-area, sunny afternoon. @Papa already CENTER-FRAME at the grill wearing @papas-apron, spatula in hand, three plump hot dogs and four juicy hamburger patties sizzling beautifully on the grates with golden grill-marks, light steam wisps. Mama's voice carries clearly from inside the house, off-screen: Mama (offscreen, calling): "Honey! The doorbell — can you grab it? My hands are full!" Papa pauses, blinks at his beautiful sizzling food, then sighs theatrically and sets the spatula down on the grill's side shelf. clips.title: Joe creeps out from the bush — silent fourth-wall wink
clips.prompt: Low-angle dramatic shot in @backyard-grill-area — the picnic table on the LEFT, the empty grill on the RIGHT, sunny afternoon. @Joe (small fluffy off-white Pomeranian, fox-like face, perky triangle ears, bushy curling tail) already CENTER-FRAME, slowly creeping out from behind the leafy bushes at the property edge, low to the ground, ears perked forward, nose twitching dramatically, his eyes laser-locked on @burger-platter (now on the picnic table loaded with three plump grilled hot dogs and four juicy grill-marked hamburger patties). Joe (a small low cheerful conspiratorial yip to himself): "Mff hff hff!" Tail wags slowly with criminal intent.

clips.title: AUDIENCE-ASK — Papa returns to empty grill, asks the camera if he dreamed it
clips.prompt: Medium shot of @backyard-grill-area, sunny afternoon. @Papa already CENTER-FRAME at the grill wearing @papas-apron, just returned from the house. The grill is COMPLETELY EMPTY — just a thin curl of dying smoke and bare grates. The picnic table behind him is also EMPTY of food. Papa stops dead in his tracks, blinks rapidly three times, looks at the empty grill, looks at the empty picnic table, looks back at the empty grill. He pats his apron pockets with both hands as if maybe the burgers are in there. He SLOWLY scratches the back of his head, deeply baffled, eyebrows knit. Then Papa turns to face the camera DIRECTLY, looks straight into the lens with a completely lost, baffled expression, palms open beside his head in confused-shrug position. Papa (to camera, totally bewildered, soft and sincere): "Wait... wait wait wait. Did I cook anything today? Or did I... did I just DREAM the whole thing?" He squints at the camera one more second, genuinely seeking guidance from the kid watching, then looks down at the empty grill and shakes his head muttering: "I was SO sure. I felt the heat. I FLIPPED a patty. ...did I?"

clips.title: Papa sees Joe — the burger graveyard reveal
clips.prompt: Wide shot of @backyard-grill-area, sunny afternoon, viewer-favorite reveal angle. @Papa already CENTER-LEFT at the grill wearing @papas-apron, mid-flip with his spatula, now turning his head sharply over his right shoulder toward the bushes corner because of an off-screen yell. @Joe already CENTER-RIGHT half-tucked into the leafy bushes at the property edge, still holding a hot dog crosswise in his mouth, fluffy chest dramatically smeared with bright red ketchup. Around Joe in the grass: the now-EMPTY @burger-platter tipped sideways, with crumpled paper wrappers scattered, a few stray bun pieces, a tiny smear of ketchup on the ceramic — a comic 'crime scene' of vanished burgers. Papa's eyes track the scene one element at a time — sees the empty platter, sees the wrappers, sees Joe. His jaw drops slowly, spatula drifts down to his side, eyebrows shoot up to his hairline in slow-motion realization. Papa (a long, low, stunned breath): "...the ENTIRE TIME?" He stares wordlessly. Joe stares back, hot dog firmly in mouth, slow innocent blink, perfect criminal poker-face.

clips.title: Family laughs together — 'Give it back, little dog!'
clips.prompt: Wide cheerful family-comedy shot in @backyard-grill-area, sunny golden-hour afternoon, soft warm kid-show comedy lighting, family-laugh-out-loud energy throughout. (BEAT 1 — 0-5s) @Papa already CENTER-LEFT in the grass on his knees, wearing @papas-apron, leaning forward laughing helplessly with both hands flat on his knees, mouth wide open in a big silly belly-laugh, eyes crinkled with pure dad-joy, NOT angry NOT scary NOT shouting. @Joe already FOREGROUND-CENTER STANDING FIRMLY ON ALL FOUR PAWS planted flat on the green grass facing the camera at a slight angle, hot dog still firmly crossways in his mouth like a cigar, big innocent eyes wide, fluffy head tilted one inch sideways, ears perked up, picture of cute confusion. Papa (booming through his laughter, gentle silly playful tone): "Oh JOE — give it BACK, little dog!" (BEAT 2 — 5-10s) @Sara already CENTER-RIGHT next to Papa, kneeling close to him in the grass, both hands cupped giggling against her cheeks. @Eva already FAR-RIGHT next to Sara, also kneeling in the grass, both arms held out wide in a friendly 'come here' welcoming gesture, giggling helplessly. Sara (singing-giggling): "Little dog, little dog!" Eva (giggling sweetly): "Give it back, silly Joe!" Joe wiggles his fluffy head playfully back and forth, hot dog still firmly in his mouth, takes one tiny playful hop-in-place on his four paws (NOT lifting off the ground), tail wagging mischievously. (BEAT 3 — 10-15s) Papa, Sara, and Eva all extend gentle laughing hands forward toward Joe in a comic 'come here you' welcoming gesture, all three still kneeling/grounded in the grass laughing together. Joe gives a tiny happy yip ("Mmf hff hff!") and gives a small playful wiggle in place, hot dog still firmly in his mouth, all four paws planted firmly on the grass throughout. Papa, Sara, Eva all dissolve into helpless warm family laughter together watching the cute pup. Cheerful, warm, kid-show family-comedy joy throughout.

clips.title: CLIFFHANGER + AUDIENCE-ASK — Papa keeps one eye on Joe, 'pancakes?!'

musicVideos.title: PARENT-ACTIVITY (15s) — Papa-tag-monster game with Sara and Eva
musicVideos.prompt: Wide cinematic playful shot in @backyard, green grass, leafy backyard tree, black iron fence, sunny golden-hour afternoon, soft cheerful kid-show comedy lighting. @Papa already CENTER-FRAME on hands and knees in the middle of the grass in goofy 'tag-monster' pose, wearing @papas-apron, big silly grin, fingers wiggling in the air playfully like a friendly cartoon monster. @Sara already on the LEFT side of the yard a few feet away in a low dodge stance, knees bent, both arms held wide for balance, mouth wide in a delighted giggle. @Eva already on the RIGHT side of the yard a few feet away in her own dodge stance, hands flapping with excitement, hair bouncing. (BEAT 1 — 0-5s) Papa wiggles his fingers and waggles his shoulders. Papa (booming silly tag-monster voice): "GRRRR! The TAG-MONSTER is HUNGRY!" Sara squeals delightedly and pivots in place. Eva bounces on her toes. Sara (giggling, one hand cupped beside her mouth): "You can't catch us, Tag-Monster!" (BEAT 2 — 5-10s) Papa LUNGES sideways toward the LEFT in slow comic slow-mo, arms still wide. Sara already PIVOTS and DUCKS to the right side, evading easily, laughing all the way. Eva dances in place on her side, clapping in delight. Papa lands on his palms in the grass with a goofy 'WOMP'. Papa (theatrically defeated, in the grass): "NOOO! Too FAST for the Tag-Monster!" (BEAT 3 — 10-15s) Papa hops back up onto knees, then LUNGES the OTHER way toward the RIGHT side. Eva already DUCKS and rolls onto the grass laughing, but Papa GENTLY taps her shoulder with one big fingertip. Eva (cheering on her back in the grass, grass-blade in her hair): "GOTCHA back, Papa!" Sara claps from her side and bounces. Papa (puffing out his chest in mock-victory): "ONE Tag-Monster point!" The three are giggling helplessly together. Cheerful, warm, kid-show energy throughout.
